title: /images/create endpoint leaks temporary files
body:
### Issue Description

The `/images/create` API endpoint leaks temporary files, prefixed with `api_load.tar`, when uploading a tar file. [CreateImageFromSrc](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/7a0de1880ad01dd75e6db30830b6dfd913e81710/pkg/api/handlers/compat/images.go#L201) creates a temporary file for this case that it doesn't clean up.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

* Start the server with `podman system service -t0 unix:///tmp/podman.sock`
* Upload an image with `curl` (any image will do, this command assumes there's a `hello.tar`):
  ```shell
  curl --unix-socket /tmp/podman.sock \
  -X POST "http://podman/images/create?fromSrc=-&repo=my-image&tag=latest" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/x-tar" \
  --data-binary @hello.tar
  ```
* Stop the server with ^C
* Look at the files in /var/tmp:
  ```shell
  ls /var/tmp | grep api_load.tar
  ```


### Describe the results you received

There is one file per `POST` to `/images/create` (when `fromSrc` is `-`).

### Describe the results you expected

There are no files left over named `api_load.tar`.

### podman info output
